mission.title: Install and Configure MetalLB (Bare-metal Load Balancer)
mission.description: MetalLB is a load-balancer implementation for bare metal Kubernetes clusters, utilizing standard routing protocols. It is ideal for environments where cloud-native load balancers are not available, providing a way to expose services externally. Key trade-offs include the need for manual configuration of IP address pools and reliance on the underlying network infrastructure.

steps.title: Add MetalLB Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official MetalLB Helm repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install MetalLB using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add metallb https://metallb.github.io/metallb

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the MetalLB repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install MetalLB
steps.description:
This step installs MetalLB in the 'metallb-system' namespace using the specified version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace metallb-system

helm install metallb metallb/metallb --namespace metallb-system --version 0.15.3

```

> Creates a namespace for MetalLB.
> Installs MetalLB with the specified version.

steps.title: Configure MetalLB
steps.description:
This step creates an IPAddressPool and L2Advertisement to define the IP address pool that MetalLB will use to allocate external IPs.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: metallb.io/v1beta1
kind: IPAddressPool
metadata:
  name: default-pool
  namespace: metallb-system
spec:
  addresses:
    - 192.168.1.240-192.168.1.250
---
apiVersion: metallb.io/v1beta1
kind: L2Advertisement
metadata:
  name: default
  namespace: metallb-system
EOF

```

> Replace the IP range with your own network's range.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: metallb.io/v1beta1
kind: IPAddressPool
metadata:
  name: default-pool
  namespace: metallb-system
spec:
  addresses:
    - 192.168.1.240-192.168.1.250
---
apiVersion: metallb.io/v1beta1
kind: L2Advertisement
metadata:
  name: default
  namespace: metallb-system
EOF

steps.title: Verify MetalLB Installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the MetalLB pods are running and ready in the metallb-system namespace.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n metallb-system

kubectl get svc -n metallb-system

```

> You should see MetalLB controller and speaker pods running.
> Verify that the MetalLB service is up and running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall MetalLB
uninstall.description:
This step removes MetalLB from your cluster. Be aware that any services using MetalLB for external access will lose their external IPs, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall metallb --namespace metallb-system

kubectl delete namespace metallb-system

```

> Uninstalls the MetalLB Helm release.
> Deletes the MetalLB namespace and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade MetalLB
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your configuration. This step will upgrade MetalLB to the latest version while ensuring the service remains available.

```bash

kubectl get ipaddresspools,l2advertisements -n metallb-system -o yaml > metallb-config-backup.yaml

helm upgrade metallb metallb/metallb --namespace metallb-system --version 0.15.3

kubectl get pods -n metallb-system

```

> Backup the current MetalLB configuration.
> Upgrades MetalLB to the specified version.
> Verify that the MetalLB pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get ipaddresspools,l2advertisements -n metallb-system -o yaml > metallb-config-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to allocate IP address'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the configured IP address range is exhausted or misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the ConfigMap for the address pool configuration and ensure there are available IPs.

troubleshooting.title: MetalLB pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the MetalLB pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n metallb-system' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Services are not getting external IPs
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate that MetalLB is not properly configured or the service type is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service type is set to 'LoadBalancer' and that MetalLB is configured correctly.

troubleshooting.title: MetalLB controller pod is not running
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The controller may fail to start due to resource constraints or configuration issues.

**Fix:**
Check the resource limits and ensure the configuration is valid.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for BGP peering, improved layer2 configuration options.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required from v0.14.

resolution.summary: You should see the MetalLB pods running with the status 'Running' and the services should have external IPs assigned when queried with 'kubectl get svc'.

    - 192.168.1.240-192.168.1.250

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster with the required tools installed.
